questions.question: What can you earn by completing a Notion Academy learning path?

options.explanation: Notion Academy does not offer monetary payments for completing paths.
The article states that completing paths helps you earn badges.
Free subscriptions are not mentioned as a reward for learning paths.
Certificates are digital and tied to the Notion Certified Admin program, not to learning paths.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a primary offering of Notion Academy in the article?

options.explanation: Earning badges is explicitly mentioned.
The certification program is highlighted in the article.

options.text: Join a community of Notion users
options.explanation: Joining the community is one of the three main offerings.
The article does not mention third‑party plugins as an Academy offering.

questions.question: If you want to learn how to build an intake system using forms, charts, and automations, which lesson should you enroll in?

options.explanation: Custom Agents focus on automating repeat work, not specifically on intake systems.

options.text: An issue tracker for any team
options.explanation: The description of this lesson mentions building an intake system with forms, charts, and automations.
Workspace design covers best practices for designing a workspace, not intake systems.
That lesson is about creating and selling templates, unrelated to intake systems.

questions.question: How many lessons are included in the "Advanced Path"?

options.explanation: The Essentials Path contains 3 lessons, not the Advanced Path.
The Workflows Path has 4 lessons.
The article lists the Advanced Path as containing 5 lessons.
No path is described as having 6 lessons in the article.

questions.question: Which lesson is categorized as "Advanced" and focuses on best practices for workspace architecture?

options.explanation: The "Workspace design" lesson is marked as Advanced and covers workspace best practices.
Building basics is a Beginner lesson.
Custom Agents is a Beginner lesson about automation.
Templates & marketplace is an Intermediate lesson.

questions.question: Which path description emphasizes "Create connected systems that unite teams and projects, with the help of AI"?

options.explanation: Essentials Path focuses on basic skills, not AI‑driven systems.
The Workflows Path description matches the quoted text.
Advanced Path focuses on automation and architecture, not specifically AI‑driven workflows.
There is no "Custom Agents Path" listed in the article.

questions.question: Which statement accurately describes the eligibility for becoming a Notion Certified Admin?

options.text: Only users on the Enterprise plan can become certified.
options.explanation: The article says any plan type is eligible.

options.text: Certification is limited to individuals working at companies with more than 500 employees.
options.explanation: No employee‑count restriction is mentioned.

options.text: Anyone, regardless of plan type or company size, can become a Notion Certified Admin after completing the certification.
options.explanation: The article explicitly states certification is available for any plan type at any company.

options.text: Only Notion employees are allowed to earn the Certified Admin badge.
options.explanation: The program is open to external users, not just employees.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the "Custom Agents" lesson as described in the article?

options.text: To teach how to build custom database relations.
options.explanation: Custom Agents focus on automation, not database relations.

options.text: To demonstrate how to schedule and trigger automations that reduce repetitive work.
options.explanation: The lesson description states Custom Agents help automate repeat work, can run on a schedule or respond to triggers.

options.text: To provide a deep dive into Notion's API for developers.
options.explanation: The article does not mention API usage in this lesson.

options.text: To guide users through creating public templates for the Marketplace.
options.explanation: Template creation is covered in a different lesson.
